name: AIM ImmunoTech Inc.
business: AIM ImmunoTech Inc. is an immuno-pharma company focused on research and development of therapeutics for cancers, viral diseases, and immune-deficiency disorders. Its primary products are Ampligen (rintatolimod), an experimental double-stranded RNA molecule, and Alferon N Injection (interferon alfa), approved for refractory genital warts. Ampligen is approved for commercial sale in Argentina for severe chronic fatigue syndrome but not yet approved in the U.S.
sellsTo: patients with cancers, viral diseases, immune disorders
clinical trial sites and early access programs
Argentine market for severe chronic fatigue syndrome
namedSuppliers: PPD clinical research business of Thermo Fisher Scientific

exposures.sentence: AIM ImmunoTech Inc. and its subsidiaries are an immuno-pharma company headquartered in Ocala, Florida, and focused on the research and development of therapeutics to treat multiple types of cancers, viral diseases and immune-deficiency disorders for which there are inadequate or unmet therapies.

catalysts.event: Agreement with PPD to design Phase 3 clinical trial for Ampligen in late-stage pancreatic cancer
catalysts.sentence: In March 2026, we announced an agreement with the PPD clinical research business of Thermo Fisher Scientific to design AIM s anticipated Phase 3 clinical trial in the use of Ampligen in the treatment of late-stage pancreatic cancer.

catalysts.event: Completion of rights offering raising approximately $1.8 million
catalysts.sentence: On March 6, 2026, we completed a rights offering (the 2026 Rights Offering ) to our stockholders and to holders of certain of our outstanding options and warrants that had the right to participate in the 2026 Rights Offering as of February 10, 2026, the record date.

catalysts.event: Reverse split of common stock authorized and effected
catalysts.sentence: On May 29, 2025, the Board authorized the Reverse Split and on June 10, 2025, the Company filed an amendment to its Articles of Incorporation effecting a reverse split of its outstanding shares of Common Stock on a one for 100 basis (the Reverse Split ).

catalysts.event: Regained compliance with NYSE American low selling price rule and trading reinstated
catalysts.sentence: On June 11, 2025, the Company was notified by the Exchange that the Company had regained compliance with Section 1003(f)(v) of the Exchange s Company Guide (low selling price) and that trading in the Company s Common Stock was reinstated on the Exchange on June 17, 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Pancreatic cancer killed more than 100,000 people in American and EU markets and more than 450,000 worldwide as recently as 2022
tamClaims.sentence: Pancreatic cancer killed more than 100,000 people in the American and European Union markets and more than 450,000 people worldwide as recently as 2022.
